The last form must also have Multiform IV (12). Each form will individually take 485 days to enchant, except the last will take 497 days. Total standard cost: 215,665sp. Level 90 item.

name: Lesser Dagger of Assassination
description: This unassuming iron dagger has a dark blade and a black leather grip. It provides a +10 OB bonus and a +5 Stalking bonus of iron with a snakeskin grip has a +15 OB and may be thrown with a range increment of 20'.
recipe: Work Lesser Metals (5), Range Improvement III (17), Weapon III (13). Days: 35. Standard cost: 675sp. Level 17 item.

name: Lightning Lance
description: This long spear is of humble appearance, with an iron head and oaken shaft of plain design. There is, however, a six-inch section of the shaft behind the head which is wrapped with copper wire (total cost 2 sp). This is a +15 weapon, and it does an additional Electrical critical one severity less than the regular critical. Twice per day, the wielder may cast Lightning Bolt I.
recipe: Work Lesser Metals (5), Lightning Bolt I (10), Embed X (19), Daily XX (40), Additional Crit II (14), Weapon III (13). Days: 101. Standard cost: 4,447sp. Level 40 item.

name: Mace of Crushing
description: This plain wood-hafted iron mace has a +25 OB bonus and does an extra Impact critical of equal severity to the normal critical. Even better, although it is a weapon for a Medium Size user, it strikes as a Big weapon.
recipe: Work Lesser Metals (5), Weapon V (50), Additional Critical III (19), Increase Size (30). Days: 104. Standard cost: 5,622sp. Level 50 item.

name: Muldek’s Rapier
description: This rapier is made of Starfall (+15 OB) and can cast Blur (Shield Mastery list) once per day.
recipe: Work Metal III (19), Blur (3), Embed III (7), Daily III (10). Days: 24. Standard cost: 926sp. Level 19 item. Ny’Calubraithe, the Long Sword of the Elven

name: Ny'Calubraithe, the Long Sword of the Elven Smiths
description: This +20 white alloy longsword with a grip of red fell beast leather has many powers. It does an extra Impact critical of the same severity as the normal critical inflicted. It is a Slaying I weapon against Elf-Demons, a Slaying II weapon against Orcs, a Slaying III weapon against Trolls, and a Slaying IV weapon against the Undead. It may be thrown with a 10' range increment. Once per day the bearer may cast Spell Bending II (Spell Reins), but only against electrical attacks (e.g., a Shock Bolt). Twice per day the bearer can cast Stun Relief III (Self Healing).
recipe: Work Mundane Metal III (12), Slaying IV (60 x 5), Slaying III (40 x 3), Stun Relief III (10), Embed X (20), Daily XX (50), Spell Bending II (8), Embed X (19), Daily X (25), Slaying I (15 x 3), Slaying II (20), Additional Crit III (19), Weapon IV (25), Range Improvement I (6). Days: 649. Standard cost: 50,844sp. The limitation on the Spell Bending is quite narrow, on a power that accounts directly for over 52 days of enchantment, dropping the market value down to 45,760 sp. Level 60 item.

name: Off-Hand Dagger
description: This low steel (+5) dagger provides a +20 bonus to Multiple Attacks, but only when used in conjunction with another weapon. It also provides the Ambidextrous talent.
recipe: Work Mundane Metal I (7), General IV (30), Talent Embedding II (10). Days: 42. Standard cost: 1,429sp. Level 30 item.

name: Parrying Falchion
description: This low steel (+5) falchion allows its wielder to cast Anticipate Attack (Anticipations) five times each day.
recipe: Work Mundane Metal I (7), Daily V (16), Embed I (3), Anticipate Attack (1). Days: 22. Standard cost: 446sp. Level 16 item.

name: Prism Sword
description: This iron arming sword has a small prism as its pommel. It has no bonus to OB, but the wielder may cast Shock Bolt I (Light Law) at will. Unfortunately, due to an imperfection in the enchantment, it must draw the energy to do so from surrounding light, so the effect only works in at least torchlight, and each use causes a 5' radius magical darkness around the user (moving with them) for 10 rounds.
recipe: Work Lesser Metals (5), Constant III (17 x 2 at will), Embed II (5), Shock Bolt I (2). Days: 46. Standard cost: 938sp. Level 17 item.

name: Ranger’s Blade
description: This high steel broadsword has been enchanted to +30 and does an additional Electrical critical equal to the normal critical. It is a +8 Spell Adder for Rangers (but only while in hand) and provides a magical Shield (Shield Mastery) at will.
recipe: Work Mundane Metal II (9), Weapon VI (75), Constant III (17 x 2 at will), Embed II (5), Shield (2), Spell Adder X (50 x 1/2 single profession), Additional Crit III (19). Days: 123. Standard cost: 9,950sp. Level 75 item.

name: Red Glowing Axe
description: This +10 hand axe of iron and ash has its range increment increased by 5' when thrown. Additionally, it will glow red along the blade if within 10' of magic (active or waiting spell or an enchanted item) outside of the bearer’s aura.
recipe: Work Lesser Metals (5), Weapon II (8), Detection (3[increased radius] x 2[broad category]), Range Improvement I (6). Days: 25. Standard cost: 255sp. Level 8 item.

name: Red Spear of Diif-Marash
description: This spear has a high steel (+10) head and a shaft of ironwood painted red. It is a Slaying II weapon against Orcs and has a 20' range increment enhancement when thrown. Any bleeding results it inflicts are increased by 1 hit per round.
recipe: Work Mundane Metal II (9), Slaying II (20), Range Improvement III (17), Vampiric Weapon (16). Days: 52. Standard cost: 1,297sp. Level 20 item.

name: Scimitar of Throwing
description: This ordinary-looking scimitar
recipe: Work Metal I (11), Daily XX (40), Embed XX (30), Haste III (20), Reduced Time II (40), Constant III (17), Embed III (7), Blur (3), Weapon IV (25). Days: 193. Standard cost: 14,900sp. Level 40 item.

name: Shieldsplitter
description: This ordinary looking hand axe with an iron head is actually a +15 weapon with the Shield Breaker ability and great utility as a throwing weapon, having its range increment increased to (25') and the ability to teleport back to the thrower after its attack.
recipe: Work Lesser Metals (5), Weapon III (13), Shield Breaker (12), Range Improvement II (11), Returning Weapon True (9). Days: 50. Standard cost: 805sp. Level 13 item.

name: Skorbaas, the Heart Seeker
description: This iron dagger has been enchanted to +15. It provides an extra 10 ranks of Ambush when used as a melee weapon and has the unique ability to make called shots against the torso (of Small, Medium or Big targets) at no penalty (Serendipitous spell failure result). It is a Slaying II weapon against Animals, Monsters, Races, and Vampires, but only against those which have hearts (limitation by spell failure).
recipe: Work Lesser Metals (5), Slaying II (20 x 5), Slaying II (20 x 5), Slaying II (20 x 5), Slaying II (20 x 3), General V (60), Weapon III (13). Days: 438. Standard cost: 31,539sp. Level 60 item.

name: Spear of Battle Spells
description: This is a +20 spear (black alloy and vaal) which is a +5 Spell Adder for spells that increase OB (including those doing so indirectly through something like boosting Strength) or melee damage. There is said to be one for each Semi Spellcaster profession, for which the correct spear acts as a x2 PP Multiplier.
recipe: Work Wood (18), Consecration (2), Sacred Adder V (19), Power Multiplier IV (25 x 1/2 single profession). Days: 32. Standard cost: 1,497sp. Level 25 item.

name: Spiritslayer
description: This low steel broadsword has been enchanted to +20, with an additional +10 OB against Extra Planar Creatures and Incorporeal Undead. It is Slaying II against Extra Planar Creatures and Slaying III against Incorporeal Undead. It has Medium Intelligence and is hostile to undead and demons, which it will note from their auras at up to 40'.
recipe: Work Mundane Metal I (7), Slaying III (40 x 3 Type), Slaying II (20 x 5 Category), Weapon IV (25), Weapon II (8 x 2 Category), Weapon II (8), Artificial Intelligence III (9). Days: 280. Standard cost: 13,770sp. Level 40 item.

name: Staff of Aristarchus
description: This ironwood (+15) quarterstaff will allow casting Dark Stunning (Dark Channels) at will and Absolution (Dark Channels) fortnightly (every two weeks). It provides Black Channel II (Dark Channels) once per month and Diabolical Skill II (Demonic Pacts) once per week.

name: Staff of Cold
description: This ash quarterstaff provides a +5 OB and does an additional cold critical of one severity less than the normal critical. It also acts as a +3 Spell Adder usable only for cold spells.
recipe: Consecration (2), Work Mundane Wood (4), Additional Crit II (14), Sacred Adder III (11), Weapon I (4). Days: 35. Standard cost: 560sp. Level 14 item.

name: Staff of Flames
description: This ash quarterstaff provides a +5 OB and does an additional heat critical of one severity less than the normal critical. It also acts as a +3 Spell Adder usable only for fire spells.
recipe: Consecration (2), Work Mundane Wood (4), Additional Crit II (14), Sacred Adder III (11), Weapon I (4). Days: 35. Standard cost: 560sp. Level 14 item.

name: Staff of Ice
description: This is a staff of ice, with a grip of wolf’s fur. A constant Freezing Cold focused on the item itself keeps it frozen. It has been made “indestructible” to allow the brittle material to endure. Pointed at one end, the ice may be used to strike as a spear or as a quarterstaff, doing an additional Cold critical of equal severity to the normal critical. The bearer of this staff gains Tier I of the Slow Bleeder talent. The staff is a +5 Spell Adder for Ice Law spells only. It provides the bearer a constant Heatarmor (Elemental Shields) effect, and it can be used to Freeze Liquid (Liquid Manipulation) at will.
recipe: Work Inorganic (13), Unbreakable Work (90), Constant X (35), Embed X (19), Heatarmor (9), Constant III (18 x 2 at will), Embed II (6), Freeze Liquid (2), Talent Embedding VI (35), Constant III (17), Embed I (3), Freezing Cold (1), Additional Crit III (19), Spell Adder V (30 x 1/10 single list). Days: 288. Standard cost: 28,512sp. Level 90 item.

name: Staff of Light
description: This quarterstaff is +10 to OB and does an additional Impact critical equal in severity to the normal critical. Furthermore, it is a magical staff allowing the casting of either Lightning Bolt I or Shock Bolt III.
recipe: Work Mundane Wood (4), Make Staff (14), Lightning Bolt I (10), Shock Bolt III (8), Logic Fork (1), Embed X (19), List Embedding (2), Additional Crit III (19), Weapon II (8). Days: 85. Standard cost: 1,955sp. Level 19 item.

name: Swiftslayer
description: This zataak longsword has been enchanted to +40 and provides its bearer with a constant Blur (Shield Mastery). It attacks in 2 AP with no penalty, and casts Haste III (Rapid Ways) once per day.
recipe: Work Mundane Metal I (7), Stat Bonus V (50), Slaying III (40), Weapon II (8). Days: 100. Standard cost: 5,459sp. Level 50 item.

name: Sword of Giant-Slaying
description: This high steel (+10) claymore has an additional +10 bonus (+20 total) and is Slaying I against Giants.
recipe: Work Mundane Metal II (9), Weapon II (8), Slaying I (15 x 3 for a Type). Days: 52. Standard cost: 1,057sp. Level 15 item.

name: Sword of Kalis Pathon
description: This appears to be a fairly normal white alloy arming sword until looked at edgeon. Then it can be seen to be ridiculously thin, practically invisible when looked at directly from the edge side. This is an “indestructible” weapon; if it were not magically so strengthened, the metal would never withstand the rigors of combat. It has been enchanted to +30 and it strikes as if it were a Size larger. It will always do Slash as its primary critical, regardless of what the attack chart says, due to its construction. It is a Doorcleaver, a Shield Breaker, an Armor Breaker, and a Weapon Breaker. The creation of the uniquely thin blade required exceptional workmanship and extra time, resulting in a base price of 320 sp (including the price of white alloy).
recipe: Work Mundane Metal III (12), Unbreakable Work (90), Weapon VI (75), Weapon Breaker (35), Increased Size (30), Armor Breaker (18), Shield Breaker (12), Doorcleaver (10). Days: 267. Standard cost: 26,753sp. Level 90 item.

name: Sword of Returning
description: A variety of sword types have been found in various Sizes with this enchantment; the price is given here as a broadsword, scimitar, or falchion (10sp). The weapon has a +10 bonus, a thrown range increment of 15', and will teleport back to the thrower’s hand after a throwing attack.
recipe: Work Lesser Metals (5), Range Improvement II (11), Returning Weapon True (9), Weapon II (8). Days: 33. Standard cost: 439sp. Level 11 item.

name: Sword of the Fallen Star
description: There are two of these swords, twins made from the same meteorite, working both at the same time (one in the morning, one in the evening). Each is a great falchion of starfall with a grip of jackalope fur, crafted with unusual artistry and with a pommel stone of jet. Such a weapon would easily fetch 500 sp without further enhancement, but it is enchanted up to +20 and is both a Shield Breaker and an Armor Breaker. As for itself, if it fails a breakage check, it may reroll the result (once per day).
recipe: Work Metal III (19), Armor Breaker (18), Resilience (15), Shield Breaker (12), Weapon IV (25). Days: 82. Standard cost: 2,878sp. Level 25 item. Work Mundane Wood (4), Constant III (18 x 2 at will), Embed II (6), Dark Stunning (2), Daily XX (50), Embed XX (35), Absolution (13), Daily X (25), Embed X (20), Diabolical Skill II (9), Daily XX (50), Embed XX (35), Black Channel III (15). Days: 300. Standard cost: 17,015sp. Level 50 item.

name: Sword of the Golden Orb
description: This broadsword is made of Inferior Bronze, with a textured metal grip, decorated in solar imagery. When in direct sunlight, the power of this blade manifests, giving it a +15 bonus as it burns redly in the reflected sunlight. It will then also do an additional Heat critical equal in severity to the normal crit done.
recipe: Work Lesser Metals (5), Weapon III (13), Additional Crit III (19). Days: 37. Standard cost: 787sp, but the limitation of direct sunlight reduces its value to 708sp. Level 19 item.

name: Tarpan Dag
description: A dark long scimitar forged of Low Steel, with a black leather grip, Tarpan Dag is a Slaying III weapon against Elves. It is enchanted to +10. If the wielder of this weapon is an Orc, he gains a +5 increase to his Strength bonus. Close inspection of the blade will reveal that it is inscribed, though it may be harder to interpret the letters of archaic Orcish insults regarding Elves. Tentacle Staff

name: Tentacle Staff
description: This is a Big staff topped by a 10' squid tentacle. It can be used to attack as a Big Grappling attack, and if the user has less skill, it will do so with a +100 skill bonus. It is too large for a Medium combatant to wield, however.
recipe: Work Organic (7), Physical Skill V (90), Talent Embedding I (6). Days: 103. Standard cost: 9,682sp. Level 90 item.

name: Thumper
description: Created for an Adept’s Troll bodyguard, this two-handed club of hickory is a weapon for a Big wielder (doing Large attacks). It has a +20 OB bonus and the wielder has a +2 bonus to Initiative when using it. It also is quite destructive to inanimate objects, possessing both the Doorcleaver and Shield Breaker abilities.
recipe: Work Mundane Wood (4), Weapon IV (25), Initiative Bonus (4 x2 for +2 bonus), Doorcleaver (10), Shield Breaker (12). Days: 59. Standard cost: 1,476sp. Level 25 item.

name: Truncheon of Command
description: This club of hickory is a +10 weapon which strikes as if it were one Size larger. The bearer of this weapon gains a +10 bonus to his Leadership skill. Furthermore, he may cast Quest once per week and True Quest (both spells from the Spirit Mastery list) once per fortnight.
recipe: Work Mundane Wood (4), Increased Size (30), Quest (11), Embed XX (30), Daily VII (18), True Quest (20), Embed XX (30), Daily VII (18), General II (10). Days: 161. Standard cost: 7,506sp. Level 30 item.

name: Whip of Beast Mastery
description: This whip is a +5 weapon, grants a +5 bonus to Animal Handling, and is a +2 Spell Adder for spells that charm, control, influence, or communicate with animals. It has a mahogany handle carved with dozens of detailed animal forms (35 sp value).
recipe: Work Organic (6), Consecration (2), General I (6 x 2 all specializations), Sacred Adder II (7), Weapon I (4). Days: 31. Standard cost: 314sp. Level 7 item.

name: Caprayi, the Eternal Wind
description: This white longbow is constructed of polished springwood with fittings of gold and adamantese (the bow would be worth 1000 sp even unenchanted). Utterly beautiful to behold, it is equally deadly. It has range increments increased by +50’ and arrows fired from it strike with a +1 Size increase and do an additional Impact critical of equal severity. Against Large or larger creatures, it is also a Slaying II weapon. This was originally the weapon of the God of the East Wind, but he gave it to a mortal champion and it passed out of mythology and into history. The Slaying power of this weapon is broader than allowed by mortal enchantments, but it has a limitation to creatures of great size, which are rarer, but more powerful. A multiplier of x10 is therefore used.
recipe: Work Organic True (20), Slaying II (20 x 10 = 200), Increase Size (30), Additional Crit III (19), Range Improvement III (17). Days: 286. Standard cost: 17,874sp. Level 50 item.

name: Flare Arrow
description: These arrows are single use items. When fired, a flare arrow will launch a Flare (Light’s Way) at the top of its trajectory. If fired upward, an arrow can reach half its maximum range. The added height given to the Flare allows it to illuminate a wider area and for a longer time.
recipe: Work Organic (6), Embed XX (35), Flare (11). Days: 52. The total PP requirement for a batch of five arrows is 76 PP, so the standard price is 1,976sp for the batch or 395sp each. Level 35 item.

name: Grazjanni Sling Stone
description: Each of these stones is a +10 missile, growing spines once slung that allows it to do an additional Puncture critical of one severity less than the normal critical. Because of these spines, the stones are effective at delivering poisons and are often used with poison applied. The spines will fall off once the stone is removed from its target (or fails to hit) and will grow anew when slung again.
recipe: Work Mundane Stone (3), Additional Crit II (14), Weapon II (8). Days: 25. Standard cost: 400sp. Level 14 item.

name: Javelin of Lightning
description: This javelin will release a Lightning Bolt I (Light Law) upon doing a critical when thrown. This destroys the javelin, giving it the appearance of turning into a lightning bolt as it is shattered by the electrical force traveling through it. The damage from the javelin itself still occurs. While the skill of the attacker is irrelevant to the lightning strike, the target is restricted to DB from armor quality or other sources that are from damage-resistance (e.g., Lightarmor), not from avoiding being hit (e.g., Blur or Quickness bonus), since it has already been determined that the javelin has hit.
recipe: Work Lesser Metals (5), Embed X (19), Lightning Bolt I (10). Days: 34. The total PP requirement for the batch is 54 PP, resulting in a standard price of 748sp or 150sp each. Level 19 item.

name: Lariat of Honesty
description: This spider-silk rope may have its looped end thrown up to 50' in order to deliver a Medium Grapple attack with a +20 bonus. Anyone caught by the rope (any grapple % greater than zero) is affected by an Honest Answer spell.
recipe: Work Organic (6), Weapon IV (25), Constant III (18 x 2 for triggered), Honest Answer (1), Embed I (3). Days: 71. Standard cost: 1,993sp. Level 25 item. Work Mundane Liquid (1), Enchanting Elixir III (9), Infuse Mood II (11). Days: 21. Total PP per day: 61. Standard Cost (produced as a batch) = 58.8sp. Level 11 item.

name: Shoola
description: A shoola is a bluish glass orb 2" in diameter (0.25 sp value). When broken, it releases a Cold Ball II (Ice Law list), centered on the shoola. They may be thrown as rocks, or used in a sling with a -5 penalty.
recipe: Work Inorganic (13), Cold Ball II (7), Embed VII (15). Days: 35. The total PP requirement for a batch is 74, resulting in a standard price of 19sp per day, or 665sp. The standard price per shoola is 133sp. Level 15 item.

name: White Bow
description: The fabled White Bow is a long bow of snow-white springwood enchanted to increase its base range increment by 50' and dealing Slaying II attacks against Fell Creatures and Demons. It provides constant Blur (Shield Mastery) and Holy Attack III (Holy Arms) effects so long as it is on one’s person, and the bearer is protected by an additional +20 DB against arrows and makes RRs against magic as if he were at least 30th level. Note that the bow string is not enchanted.
recipe: Work Wood (18), Consecration (2), Slaying II (20 x 5 Category), Slaying II (20 x 5 Category), Constant X (40), Embed VII (17), Holy Attack III (7), Mutual Aura II (40), Constant III (17), Embed III (7), Blur (3), Range Improvement III (17), Sacred Protection IV (14). Days: 362. Standard cost: 31,742sp. Level 75 item.

name: Woodman’s Bow of Good Aiming
description: This English yew (+10' range increment) longbow can be fired as a 1 AP action without penalty. Once per day, it allows the casting of Aiming (Essence Hand).
recipe: Work Organic (6), Daily X (25), Embed X (19), Aiming (10), Reduced Time II (40). Days: 90. Standard cost: 4,070sp. Level 40 item.

name: Flenatir’s Potion
description: This powerful potion allows the imbiber to take on a natural AT of 5 and fly on temporarily grown wings with a base speed twice his walking speed (see Foul Transformations list for more details). The effect lasts for somewhat over 12 hours (another 30 minutes for the armor, another 300 minutes for the wings, the Greater Extension in the combination lifting all spells to a casting level of 30). These expensive, but highly useful, potions frequently come in a bottle marked with a red minotaur. Flenatir trades them for various favors, and some few of these are traded on or captured.
recipe: Work Mundane Liquid (1), Make Potion True (30), Wings III (14, Foul Transformations), Greater Extension (30, Spell Enhancement), Armoring IV (11, Foul Transformations), Greater Extension (30). Days: 116. The total PP requirement is 240 PP, resulting in a standard cost of 4,408sp for the batch. This results in a standard cost per potion of 882sp. Level 30 potion.

name: Gaseous Potion of Sleep V
description: A gaseous potion has the effect of the spell being cast separately on each individual within the radius, so rather than Sleep V (on the Spirit Mastery list) distributing its effects over five levels worth of targets as it would in a single normal casting, the gaseous potion will affect every individual within the potion’s 3' radius unless they are sixth level or above (a successful RR against 3rd level Essence magic will also protect them).
recipe: Work Mundane Gas (5), Make Potion III (3), Gaseous Potion (13), Sleep V (3). Days = 24. PP per day = 56. Standard Cost = 76.8 sp (batch created, rounded on the table to 77sp). Level 13 item.

name: Mood Potion
description: A Psychographer can create an elixir that makes the drinker feel a particular mood (which must be selected at the time the potion is created), such as calm, happiness, amorousness, or despair. An RR modified as for Fear (i.e., using SD) against an 11th level effect is required to not act in accord with the mood, actions in accordance gaining +10 while those counter to the mood suffer a -10 modifier.

name: Potion of Magical Competency
description: Any enchantment that causes the wearer/bearer of an item to be subjected to an effect, rather than the item itself (which excludes Weapon spells, for example), is suitable for inclusion via Enchanting Elixir (subject to GM approval). So, working with an Adept with access to the Enchantment Mastery list, a Potion of Magical Competency can be brewed. The imbiber of this potion gains a +10 bonus to all SCRs for the next hour.
recipe: Work Mundane Liquid (1), Enchanting Elixir II (3), Casting Focus II (7). Days: 11. Total PP per day: 27. Standard Cost (produced as a batch) = 19.8sp.

name: Potion of Mightiness
description: This is an enchanting elixir, created by a Psychographer. If the imbiber's Strength stat bonus is less than +10, it is raised to +10 for one hour following the consumption of this potion.
recipe: Work Mundane Liquid (1), Enchanting Elixir II (3), Fixed Bonus III (13). Days: 13. Total PP per day: 33. Standard Cost per potion (produced as a batch) = 51sp. Level 13 item.

name: Potion of Nightvision
description: This potion provides Nightvision (from the Physical Enhancement list, level 4) cast at 4th level and therefore lasting 40 minutes.
recipe: Work Mundane Liquid (1), Make Potion VI (9), Nightvision (4). Days = 14. PP per day = 54. Standard cost = 33.6sp each (batch created, rounded on the table to 34sp). Level 9 item.

name: Potion of Power Restoration
description: This potion allows the imbiber to immediately recover up to 20% of their maximum PP. It comes in two varieties. The lesser version will only restore the PP of a caster of the realm of Essence. The greater version will restore PP to anyone.
recipe: Work Mundane Liquid (1), Distill Essence (7). Days: 8. Total PP per day: 40. Standard cost per potion (produced as a batch): 16sp. Level 7 item. Note that Distill Essence must be cast on each dose. Greater version: Work Mundane Liquid (1), Distill Essence True (14). Days: 15. Total PP per day: 75. Standard cost per potion (produced as a batch): 51sp. Level 14 item.

name: Potion of Trollskin
description: A Psychographer can grant unusual abilities if they have a suitable template to work with. If the imbiber's intrinsic AT is less than 4, it is raised to 4 for fifteen minutes following the consumption of this potion.
recipe: Work Mundane Liquid (1), Enchanting Elixir I (1), Talent Embedding III (14). Days: 16. Total PP per day: 24. Standard Cost (produced as a batch) = 51.2sp. Level 14 item.

name: Access Book
description: This appears as a slender, portable religious text, and it is. (Minimum value: 2 sp.) However, it is also a method of gaining a knowledge of passwords, countersigns, and the like. Any approach within 100' of a guard or checkpoint will trigger a Commune True (Communal Ways) which will cause the password (or similar information) for the guard/ checkpoint to appear on a certain page. The word or phrase thus written will disappear once read.
recipe: Work Organic (6), Constant XX (90 x 2 triggered), Embed XX (35), Commune True (20). Days: 241. Standard cost: 23,620sp. Level 90 item.

name: Akarak, Slayer of Fire Spirits
description: This oak rod casts Water Bolt I (Water Law) with the bolts enchanted to be Slaying I against fire elementals.
recipe: Work Mundane Wood (4), Make Rod (10), Slaying I (15 x 2 for elemental bolt x 3 for creature Type x 1/2 for subgroup), Embed IV (9), Water Bolt I (4). Days: 72. Standard cost: 1,336sp. Level 15 item.

name: Anvil of Forging
description: This anvil provides significant benefits when incorporated into a metalcrafting workshop. It will provide up to 10 levels of spells per day from the Inorganic Skills list and is a +3 Spell Adder for Adapts.
recipe: Work Lesser Metals (5), Daily X (25), Embed X (19), List Embedding (2), Logic Fork (1), Metal Analysis (1), Stone Analysis (2), Work Mundane Stone (3), Create Alloy (4), Work Lesser Metals (5), Metallic Wand (6), Work Mundane Metal I (7), Work Mundane Gemstone (8), Work Mundane Metal II (9), Work Stone (10), Spell Adder III (14 x 1/2 single profession x 2 hands free). Days: 121. Standard cost: 3,645sp. Level 25 item.

name: Bands of Tumbling
description: This pair of bright cloth armbands add +10 to all Gymnastic maneuvers when worn and allow the wearer to cast Landing (Lofty Bridge) once a day.
recipe: Work Mundane Cloth (2), General II (10 x 4 for skill category), Daily III (10), Embed II (5), Landing (2). Days: 59. Standard cost: 767sp. Level 10 item.

name: Bell of Awe-Inspiring
description: This hand bell of silver (15 sp) is decorated with small, but elegant scenes of gods and goddesses performing acts of creation. The first time each day which this bell is rung, the ringer is provided with a True Aura (Light’s Way list) and this is backed up with an hour (equal in duration to the True Aura) of producing a mood of awe (±20 effect), as described in the Mood Impressions list, in all others within 20' of the bearer.
recipe: Work Lesser Metals (5), True Aura (6), Triggered Item (9), Embed VII (17), Daily X (25), Infuse Mood IV (25), Conditional Mood (4), Projected Mood IV (16). Days: 107. Standard cost: 3,118sp. Level 25 item.

name: Bell of Healing
description: This small golden bell with a handle in the form of a rearing unicorn (80 sp) is a +3 Spell Adder for healing spells only. When rung, it creates a peaceful mood (±10 effect) in a 20' radius.
recipe: Work Precious Metals (6), Consecration (2), Infuse Mood II (11), Conditional Mood (4), Projected Mood IV (16), Sacred Adder III (11). Days: 50. Standard cost: 1,030sp. Level 16 item.

name: Bell of Stunning
description: This is a silver bell (20 sp) decorated with spiral designs. Up to five times each day, the wielder of this bell may cast Stunning Song (Controlling Songs), playing the bell as his performance.
recipe: Work Lesser Metals (5), Daily XX (40), Embed IV (9), Stunning Song (4). Days: 58. Standard cost: 2,514sp. Level 40 item.

name: Blanket of Warming
description: This blanket has a constant Rain Deflection (Weather Ways) and a constant Resist Cold (Elemental Shields) effect, assuring a warm, dry place of rest for the user.
recipe: Work Mundane Cloth (2), Constant X (40), Embed V (14), Rain Deflection (5), Constant III (17), Embed III (7), Resist Cold (3). Days: 88. Standard cost: 3,872sp. Level 40 item.

name: Bole of Transport
description: casts Mass Teleport (level 30 on the Lofty Bridge spell list). It requires
recipe: Work Mundane Wood (level 4) and Make Bole (level 25) to create the base item, Mass Teleport itself, and Embed True (level 50) to actually embed the spell. Thus the Adept creating it must expend 109 PP per day (79 if someone else casts the Mass Teleport) over 109 days in order to create this item. It will cost the Adept 5,450sp (plus cost of the bole itself, assume oak for 80sp) in materials alone, while the market value of the bole will be 5,966sp. If the bole was made of dask, Work Wood (level 18) would be required, increasing the required time to 123 days and raising the cost of enchantment to 6,150sp, while the wood of the bole itself would be an additional 800sp base (but increasingly expensive as one got further from the dask forests) for a total of 7,565sp.

name: Boots of Agility
description: These bright green boots with silver ornaments (8 sp) add +2 to the wearer’s Agility stat bonus.
recipe: Work Mundane Leather (3), Stat Bonus II (19). Days: 22. Standard cost: 470sp. Level 19 item.

name: Boots of Escape
description: These boots are commonly found in Small or Medium size, and will fit any humanoid of the correct Size. The wearer can cast a combined Dash and Spider’s Walk (Movement Mastery) once each day.
recipe: Work Organic (6), Automatic Sizing Organic (6), Embed True (50), List Embedding (2), Daily L (75), Spider’s Walk (12), Dash (35). Days: 186. Standard cost: 15,066sp. Level 75 item.

name: Boots of Landing
description: The wearer of these green leather boots takes no damage from falls of up to 40' and reduces the effective height of falls from greater heights by 40'. They fit any feet of the right Size and have been found in Small, Medium, and Big.
recipe: Automatic Sizing Organic (6), Work Mundane Leather (3), Landing (2, Lofty Bridge), Embed II (5), Constant III (17). Days: 33. Standard cost: 627sp. Level 17 item.

name: Boots of Leaping
description: The wearer of these red leather boots may make a vertical leap of 20' or a lateral leap of 50' each round. They fit any feet of the right Size and have been found in Small, Medium, and Big.
recipe: Automatic Sizing Organic (6), Work Mundane Leather (3), Leaping (1, Lofty Bridge), Embed I (3), Constant III (17). Days: 30. Standard cost: 570sp. Level 17 item.

name: Boots of Light Feet
description: These blue leather boots fit any humanoid feet of the right Size (and they have been found in Small, Medium, and Big), allow the wearer to use Leaping or Landing (Lofty Bridge list) once each round, prevent foot odor, provide a +50 RR bonus against fungal or parasitic attacks on the feet. If worn while recovering from a foot or ankle injury, they halve the recovery time.
recipe: Work Mundane Organic Material (2), Magic Shoes (3), Lightfoot Shoes (11). Days: 16. Standard cost: 208sp. Level 11 item. Note: These boots demonstrate the power of specialization when compared to the Boots of Landing or Boots of Leaping above. These boots are superior to either at approximately half the cost. This also demonstrates that there are two possibilities once such specialization appears: (1) the generalists stop competing in the area of specialization, so the generalist items stop being made, or (2) the specialist secrets are closely guarded, the items priced so as to leave consumer options (say, 1,000sp for these boots), and a great profit goes to the specialists without entirely driving out the generalists.

name: Boots of Silence
description: These dark brown leather boots add +20 to the wearer’s Stalking maneuvers and allow the casting of Sphere of Silence I (Sound Molding) thrice a day. They fit any Medium humanoid feet.
recipe: Work Mundane Leather (3), Automatic Sizing Organic (6), General IV (30), Daily VII (18), Embed II (5), Sphere of Silence I (2). Days: 64. Standard cost: 2,112sp. Level 30 item.

name: Boots of Speed
description: These boots allow the wearer to cast up to 20 PP per day of spells from the Rapid Ways list up to 7th level. They will fit any humanoid feet of the right Size (and they have been found in Small, Medium, and Large).
recipe: Work Organic (6), Automatic Sizing Organic (6), Daily XX (40), Logic Fork (1), List Embedding (2), Embed VII (15), Swiftness II (7), Haste I (6), Instant Spell Prep (5), Swiftness I (4), Speed I (3), Rapid Steps (2), Ready Weapon (1). Days: 98. Standard cost: 4,312sp. Level 40 item.

name: Boots of Wind Walking
description: These fine (3 sp) boots of blue leather and brass provide Wind Drift (Movement) twice per day.
recipe: Work Mundane Leather (3), Work Lesser Metals (5), Daily X (25), Embed IV (12), Wind Drift (4). Days: 49. Standard cost: 1,375sp. Level 25 item.

name: Boots of Yark
description: These Medium-sized brown leather boots fit any wearer. In addition to all the powers of Boots of Light Feet, these boots also provide a constant Sprint spell (Movement Mastery list) and allow the casting of Fly II (Lofty Bridge) once per day.
recipe: Work Mundane Organic Material (2), Magic Shoes (3), Constant True (90), Embed XX (30), Sprint (18), Fly II (7), Embed VII (15), Daily VII (18), Lightfoot Shoes (11). Days: 194. Standard cost: 18,627sp. Level 90 item.

name: Capacious Storage
description: A common category of item, these backpacks and boxes are enchanted to hold more than they appear, reduce the weight of their contents, protect those contents from harm, and permit the possessor to immediately find whatever contents they are looking for.
recipe: Work Mundane Leather (3), Isolate Contents (8), Self-Organizing Storage (14), and both Enhance Portability (17) and Dimensional Relativity (13) are cast one, two, three, or four times depending on the version. All versions are Level 17 items. Minor: As a backpack, holds 2 cu.ft. (double normal), at half weight. Days: 55. Standard cost: 1,100sp. A box with the same enchantments takes one additional day (based on Work Mundane Wood in place of Work Mundane Leather) and increases the price to 1,120sp. Lesser: Holds 4 cu.ft., one quarter weight. Days: 85. Standard cost: 1,785sp. A box adds one day and costs 1,806sp. Moderate: Holds 8 cu.ft., one eighth weight. Days: 115. Standard cost: 2,415sp. A box adds one day and costs 2,436sp. Greater: Holds 16 cu.ft., one sixteenth weight. Days:145. Standard cost: 3,190sp. A box adds one day and costs 3,212sp.

name: Cape of the Black Hand
description: This stylish dark grey cloak (1 sp) bears a life-sized silhouette of a right hand in black in the lower right corner. Once per day, the wearer may cast Darkness I (from Light Law) linked with Darkvision (from Physical Enhancement). This provides two hours of a 10' radius magical darkness around the wearer, through which he can see (as well as seeing through any other darkness to 10').
recipe: Work Mundane Cloth (2), Darkvision (12), Embed XX (30), Darkness I (6), Embed VI (13), Daily XX (40). Days: 103. Standard cost: 4,533sp. Level 40 item.

name: Cloak of Cloaking
description: This cloak allows the wearer to cast Cloaking I twice per day and gives a +15 Stalking bonus.
recipe: Work Mundane Cloth (2), General III (15), Embed IV (12), Cloaking I (4, Cloaking), Daily X (25). Days: 58. Standard cost: 1,624sp. Level 25 item.

name: Cloak of Darkness
description: This is a x1.5 PP Multiplier and a +3 Spell Adder for Evil spellcasters. It adds 10 ranks (does not increase skill bonus) to Running (which improves passive dodging). If anyone not an Evil spellcaster puts on the cloak, this triggers a Demonic Possession II (Mind Domination) spell, cast on the wearer.
recipe: Work Mundane Cloth (2), Constant X (40 x 2 triggered), Embed X (20), Demonic Possession II (10), General V (60), Spell Adder III (14 x 3 universal x 1/3 Evil), Power Multiplier II (12 x 3 x 1/3). Days: 198. Standard cost: 13,068sp. Level 60 item.

name: Cloak of Stealth
description: The wearer of this cloak gains a +20 bonus to Stalking skill.
recipe: Work Mundane Cloth (2), General IV (30). Days: 32. Standard cost: 1,024sp. Level 30 item.

name: Collar of Animal Speech
description: This adjustable collar will fit any animal of a given Size (roll randomly on 1d100: 1-5, Diminutive; 6-25, Tiny; 26-60, Small; 61-80, Medium; 81-90, Big; 91-97, Large; 98-00, Huge). The animal wearing the collar will be able to speak and unCapacious Storage derstand the native language of a person attuned to the collar. This does not otherwise change the intelligence or personality of the animal. These items are produced by a sect of Sanctifiers that worship animal spirits and learn a variant Divine Touch list where Sacred Script is replaced with a spell that produces this effect, called Animal Speech. The collars are decorative, with a minimum cost of 10 sp.
recipe: Consecration (2), Animal Speech (6). Days: 8. Standard cost: 74sp. Level 6 item.

name: Collar of Unpain
description: A collar of eog (450 sp) that can be worn with any armor, this device will negate 30% of criticals to the head. Additionally, the wearer can cast Unpain II (Body Reins) once per day.
recipe: Work Metal VI (50), Unpain II (9), Embed X (20), Daily X (25), Crit Negation VI (30). Days: 134. Standard cost: 7,820sp. Level 50 item.

name: Daywalking Cloak
description: Usually a simple black, brown, or gray hooded cloak, these items are used by many important members of the sun-sensitive Races. It provides constant protection as the Resist Light spell (Elemental Shields list). They may be found in various Sizes, to fit the various Races that need such protection.
recipe: Work Mundane Cloth (2), Resist Light (1), Embed I (3), Constant III (17). Days: 23. Standard cost: 437sp. Level 17 item.

name: Delving Text of Yol
description: This large book (5 sp) serves as a magical reference on items. It allows casting each of the following spells once per week: Armor Analysis (Armor Enchantment), Fluid Analysis (Fluid Skills), Item Analysis (General Enchantment), Metal Analysis (Inorganic Skills), Stone Analysis (Inorganic Skills), Machine Analysis (Mechanical Mastery), Weapon Analysis (Weapon Enchantment).
recipe: Work Organic (6), Daily V (14), Embed V (11), Item Analysis (5), Daily III (10), Embed II (5), List Embedding (2), Metal Analysis (1), Stone Analysis (2), Daily III (10), Embed II (5), Fluid Analysis (2), Daily I (6), Embed I (3), Armor Analysis (1), Daily I (6), Embed I (3), Machine Analysis (1), Daily I (6), Embed I (3), Weapon Analysis (1). Days: 103. Standard cost: 1,859sp. Level 14 item.

name: Dervish Drum
description: This small drum uses mastodon hide and ivory in its construction, as well as wood. It is painted with the symbols of a long-lost culture. (Value is 45 sp without considering historical significance.) It is a +25 instrument and a x 1.5 PP Multiplier for Monks. The attuned user can play the drum to produce a Dancing Song (Controlling Songs) up to three times each day.
recipe: Work Organic (6), General V (60), Daily X (25), Embed III (7), Dancing Song (3), Power Multiplier II (12 x 1/2 single profession). Days: 107. Standard cost: 6,893sp. Level 60 item.

name: Desert Suit
description: These desert robes have been enchanted to provide a constant Hues (Nature’s Guises) effect. Unfortunately, this effect only works when in a desert environment, so its value will drop considerably outside of such areas.
recipe: Work Mundane Cloth (2), Constant III (18), Embed I (3), Hues (1). Days: 24. Standard cost: 480sp. Level 18 item.

name: Dragonwand
description: This is a wand of ebony (+25 to item Strength), carved to look like a dagger (though it has no edge and would make an ineffective weapon). It would be worth 15 sp as an art object, but its magical powers are of far greater worth. Merely bearing this item protects the bearer with a constant True Armor (Elemental Shields). The wand has the power of a staff, allowing the use of any of the following spells: Fire Bolt I (Fire Law), Fire Ball (Fire Law), Lightning Bolt I (Light Law), or Fly II (Lofty Bridge).
recipe: Work Mundane Wood (4), Make Staff (14), Size Reduction (13), Size Reduction (13), Constant True (90), Embed True (50), True Armor (50), Embed X (19), List Embedding (2), Fire Bolt I (6), Fire Ball (8), Embed X (19), Lightning Bolt I (10), Embed VII (15), Fly II (7), Logic Fork (1). Days: 321. Standard cost: 32,115sp. Level 90 item. Eye Staff

name: Eggs of Divination
description: These eggs are occasionally laid by sacred birds, usually those in the vicinity of an exceptionally devout follower of the faith associated with the bird. They are immediately distinguishable from ordinary eggs by their luminescent royal purple color. When cracked open, the egg releases a spirit, which will answer a single yes/no question, as a Commune I (Communal Ways) spell. This is a low-power example of a “fallen from heaven” type item which is easily given a price by calculating an Adept-produced equivalent.
recipe: Work Organic (6), Embed XX (35), Commune I (12). Days: 53. Total PP per day: 77. Standard cost: 403sp each if produced as a batch. Level 35 item. Eggs of Divination

name: Elven Cloak
description: An Elven Cloak is gray, hooded, and light. It provides constant Resist Cold (Elemental Shields list) and Hues (Nature’s Guises list) when worn.
recipe: Work Mundane Cloth (2), Resist Cold (3), Embed III (7), Constant III (17), Hues (1), Embed I (3), Constant III (18). Days: 51. Standard cost: 1,020sp. Level 17 item.

name: Eye Staff
description: This staff is four feet of wood ending in two feet of tentacle tipped with a large eye (weight 3 lbs., Str 60). The tentacle was taken from an octopus, the eye from a sphinx. This bizarre design has been evaluated at 80 sp. This staff can be used to invoke a number of spells: Lightning Bolt I (Light Law), See Invisible I (Invisible Ways), Long Eye I (Essence’s Perceptions) or Darkvision (Senses). The eye will sway on the tentacle when within 25' of a trap and glow when within 25' of a secret door.
recipe: Work Organic (6), Make Staff (14), Embed X (19), Lightning Bolt I (10), Embed X (19), See Invisible I (9), Embed VII (15), Long Eye I (7), Embed X (19), Darkvision (9), Logic Fork (1), Detection (6), Detection (6). Days: 140. Standard cost: 3,440sp. Level 19 item.

name: Fireball Thrower
description: This is a bronze rod with the likeness of a small hand at one end, curved as if grasping a ball (10 lbs. of inferior bronze, 100 sp). Six times per day, it will fling a Fire Ball (Fire Law) from the rod’s “hand.” It has a range of 200' thanks to a Ranging I constant effect on the item. An Elemental Power IV applied to the item gives the fire balls a +20 OB. Although it is in the shape of a rod, this is a daily and constant item, and is limited in uses per day but not to a fixed number of charges.
recipe: Work Lesser Metals (5), Daily L (75), Embed X (19), Fire Ball (8), Constant X (35), Embed V (19), Ranging I (8), Elemental Power IV (18). Days: 187. Standard cost: 15,247sp. Level 75 item.

name: Flute of the Fair Elves
description: This +10 wooden flute is a +1 Spell Adder for Bards and its sound carries twice as far as a normal flute. Double all effects in the hands of an Elf (i.e., +20 bonus to maneuvers to play it, +2 Bard Spell Adder, sound carries four times as far).
recipe: Work Mundane Wood (4), Sounding II (2), Embed II (5), Constant III (17), General II (10), Spell Adder I (5 x 1/2 for single Profession). Days: 41. Standard cost: 820sp. Elves may pay half again as much for this item (1,230sp). The doubled effects are the result of serendipitous spell failure. Level 17 item.

name: Forest Mask of the Wood Elves
description: This wooden mask with leather straps is a mix of various green hues and the irregular contours of its form give it an unearthly look. Regarded as an art piece alone, it is extraordinary and might fetch in the range of 80 sp. However, it also has two powers from the Nature’s Guises list: a constant Self Cloaking when within 50' of a tree, plus the use of Plant Façade twice per day.
recipe: Work Organic (6), Daily XX (50), Embed X (20), Plant Façade (8), Constant X (40), Embed V (14), Self Cloaking (5). Days: 143. Standard cost: 7,945sp. The limitation on Self Cloaking will affect utility when actually negotiating a price. In woodlands, this is not a serious restriction, but in a desert, this item is essentially a curiosity, not a practical device. Level 50 item.

name: Gauntlet of Healing
description: This beautiful azure and silver gauntlet (180 sp value) for the right hand is a +5 Spell Adder for healing spells only. It also provides a +10 bonuses to both Medicine and Herbalism maneuvers. Finally, it casts Staunch Wound VI (Blood Law) once per day. It is a holy relic of much renown, and unauthorized use is sure to draw attention.
recipe: Consecration (2), Sacred Spell True (75), Staunch Wound VI (15), Sacred Adder (19), Sacred Skill II (8), Sacred Skill II (8). Days: 127. Standard cost: 10,340sp. Level 75 item.

name: Gloves of Subdual
description: These black leather gloves provide a +15 bonus to Subdual when used in conjunction with an Unarmed attack.
recipe: Work Mundane Leather (3), General III (15). Days: 18. Standard cost: 306sp. Level 15 item.

name: Gloves of Unbarring Ways
description: These leather gloves allow the wearer to cast up to 20 levels of spells each day off of the Unbarring Ways list up to level 5.
recipe: Work Mundane Leather (3), Embed V (11), List Embedding (2), Daily XX (40), Logic Fork (1), Lock (1), Magic Lock (2), Locklore (3), Opening I (4), Traplore (5). Days: 72. Standard cost: 3,096sp. Level 40 item.

name: Janik’s Belt of Movement
description: This broad leather belt with a bold brass buckle (1 sp) allows the caster to cast Leaping three times per day and Landing and Fly I (all Lofty Bridge) once each per day, all cast at 5th level.
recipe: Work Lesser Metals (5), Leaping (1), Landing (2), Fly I (5), List Embedding (2), Embed V (11), Daily X (25). Days: 51. Standard cost: 1,429sp. Level 25 item.

name: King’s Flagon
description: This is a golden chalice finely worked with elaborately detailed scenes appropriate to the interests of the monarch or other notable for which it was made (typical value: 500 sp). Although a magnificent status symbol, it is also of practical value, with a constant Sustenance Purification (Purifications) effect on its contents. Furthermore, an attuned user may also control the temperature of the contents to his tastes due to a constant Boil/Freeze Liquid (Liquid Alteration) effect.
recipe: Work Lesser Metals (5), Boil/Freeze Liquid (1), Hybrid Embedding (2), Embed I (3), Constant III (18), Sustenance Purification (1), Embed I (3), Constant III (18). Days: 51. Standard cost: 1,571sp. Level 18 item. King’s Flagon

name: Minstrel Cloak
description: This cloak is of fine silk (2.5 sp), silver-gray in color. It is said by some to have been discarded or lost by a god of music; others say it was a gift to the god’s long-ago favorite. This cloak is a +2 spell adder for Bards, but more notably, it provides a constant Music (Sound Control) effect, except that the music automatically adjusts to be appropriate for whatever the wearer’s situation (though the “sneakysneaky” music for stalking may be inconvenient). If the wearer sings or plays an instrument or simply hums, the cloak will join in harmony.
recipe: Work Mundane Cloth (2), Music (10), Embed X (19), Constant X (35), Spell Adder II (9 x 1/2 single profession x 2 wearable). Days: 75. Standard cost: 2,853sp. Level 35 item.

name: Montebanc Cape
description: This brilliant blue cape is a +3 Spell Adder for Dabblers and allows the wearer to Long Door I (Lofty Bridge) once per day.
recipe: Work Mundane Cloth (2), Daily X (25), Embed X (19), Long Door I (8), Spell Adder III (15 x 1/2 for single profession x 2 wearable). Days: 69. Standard cost: 1,932sp. Level 25 item.

name: Nerro’s Displacer
description: This dark gray cloak provides a constant Displacement II (Guises) effect on the wearer. The wearer may also cast Leaving I (Lofty Bridge) three times per day.
recipe: Work Mundane Cloth (2), Displacement II (10), Embed X (19), Constant X (35), Leaving I (3), Embed III (7), Daily X (25). Days: 101. Standard cost: 3,939sp. Level 35 item.

name: Pauper’s Purse
description: This battered-looking leather purse always looks empty, whether examined from the outside or opened to peer within. It also sounds empty, never jingling with coins, no matter how many it may actually carry. It can carry quite a bit, as well, for it holds eight times the volume that its size would indicate and the weight of any contents are reduced to one-quarter actual weight.
recipe: Work Mundane Leather (3), Constant X (35), Embed VI (13), Sound Glamour (6, Sound Molding), Constant X (35), Embed VI (13), Light Glamour (6, Light Molding), Enhance Portability (13), Enhance Portability (13), Dimensional Relativity (17), Dimensional Relativity (17), Dimensional Relativity (17). Days: 188. Standard cost: 7,708sp. Level 35 item.

name: Pot of Battle Smoke
description: This small clay jar (1 pound weight) may be thrown with a range increment of 50'. When it breaks, it casts a Thick Fog V (Gas Manipulation) spell that has been spellmastered to billow and smell like smoke (although it is harmless). It is often effective in making an enemy believe a fire has started, adding confusion and possibly even panic to the cover provided by the fog.
recipe: Work Inorganic (13), Embed VII (17), Thick Fog V (7). Days: 37. Total PP required: 89. Standard cost: 189sp each. Level 17 item.

name: Quill of Spell-Scribing
description: A fine feather quill typically from a goose (though showier choices have been made), this item can write one Rune II each day. Investing in a good supply of rune paper is advised for the owner of this implement.
recipe: Work Organic (6), Rune II (6, Rune Mastery), Embed VI (13), Daily VII (18). Days: 43. Standard cost: 903sp. Level 18 item.

name: Rod of Animating the Dead
description: This rod of ebony is capped with silver at both ends (20 sp). It casts Animate Dead II (Necromantic Ways). It also functions as a +3 Spell Adder for Evil spellcasters.
recipe: Work Mundane Wood (4), Make Rod (10), Animate Dead II (4), Embed IV (9), Spell Adder III (14 x 3 universal x 1/3 Evil only). Days: 41. Standard cost: 717sp. Level 14 item.

name: Rod of Exchange
description: This mysterious device comes from another dimension. It is a 2' rod of an unknown red metal, weighing three pounds. As a curiosity, it would be valued at 500 sp. It has, however, practical application, because when two people both grip the rod, they can exchange power points (regardless of realm), hits, and thoughts. Normal maximums apply to hits and PP after exchange. This device can only be approximated with spells, but we can get close enough to provide a price. Exchanging thoughts and power points may be covered by Mind Merge I (Mind Merge). The range is reduced to touch, but the ability to exchange PP across realms can be useful. The exchange of hits is just a limited Transference effect from the Healer’s Cleansing list. We will take this limitation to balance the advantageous PP exchange allowed for purposes of evaluating a price.
recipe: Work Lesser Metals (5), Constant X (40 x 2 “at will”), Embed VII (17), Mind Merge I (6), Constant III (18 x 2 “at will”), Embed II (6), Hybrid Embedding (2), Transference (2). Days: 154. Standard cost: 7,430sp. Level 40 item. Quill of Spell Scribing

name: Rod of Melting
description: The Rod of Melting is a 3' rod of dask wood carved with patterns of flame (worth 450 sp due to artistry). Each charge may be used to cast either Heat Solid or Fire Bolt I (Fire Law). Although rod-sized, it has the power of a staff (and will require Charge Staff to recharge). Because it is made of dask, it will have 150 charges at full capacity.
recipe: Work Wood (18), Make Staff (14), Size Reduction (13), List Embedding (2), Logic Fork (1), Embed VI (13), Fire Bolt I (6), Heat Solid (5). Days: 72. Standard cost: 1,962sp. Level 18 item.

name: Rope of Entanglement
description: This 60' length of spider-silk rope has a constant Enchanted Rope spell on it, and the attuned user can, at a range of up to 90', mentally command the rope to attack the nearest target as a +74 Medium Grapple.
recipe: Work Organic (6), Constant III (18), Embed I (3), Hybrid Embedding (2), Enchanted Rope (1), Increase Power (16), Trapping (16). Days: 62. Standard cost: 1,307sp. Level 18 item.

name: Scarab of Absolution
description: This large topaz is carved in the form of a beetle, with a flat underside into which are scratched several strange runes (350 carats, 2100 sp value). Once per week, it can cast Absolution (Dark Channels), triggered by being touched by (or touched to) any vertebrate that is not an Evil Cleric. For an Evil Cleric, it serves as a x2 PP multiplier.
recipe: Work Mundane Gemstone (8), Absolution (13), Embed XX (35), Daily X (25), Triggered Item (9), Power Multiplier IV (25 x 1/2 single profession x 1/3 Evil only x 2 need only be kept on person). Days: 99. Standard cost: 5,961sp. Level 35 item.

name: Scarab of Poisons
description: Usually carved from yellow or green stone, these imitation beetles (typically about 0.6 sp value, though better work and materials are not unknown) provide the bearer with a +10 bonus to Poison Mastery maneuvers and a +5 bonus to RRs against poison. Finally, the bearer can cast
recipe: Work Poison II (Fluid Skills) once per day. Work Mundane Stone (3), Daily X (25), Embed X (19), Work Poison II (10), General II (10), General I (6 x 1/2 poison only). Days: 70. Standard cost: 1,961sp. Level 25 item.

name: Scroll of Knowledge
description: This vellum scroll with ivory rollers (2.5 pounds, 120 sp value) is a +4 Spell Adder which can be used only for Informational spells. The scrolls are carved with the images of animals associated with memory and wisdom (e.g., elephants and owls).
recipe: Consecration (2), Sacred Adder IV (15). Days: 17. Standard cost: 409sp. Level 15 item.

name: Selkar’s Girdle
description: This broad belt is of stained and worn brown leather, but possesses a large, bright gold buckle (400 sp). It grants a +5 Knack for Unarmed combat (if the wearer does not already have such a Knack), the Pressing The Advantage Tier II talent for Wrestling, and provides a +60 Wrestling skill if the wearer’s own skill is less. (This is a Psychographer creation and uses Psychographer Work spells.)
recipe: Work Precious Metals (6), Physical Skill IV (35), Knack (12), Talent Embedding II (10). Days: 63. Standard cost: 2,794sp. Level 35 item.

name: Shimmering Cloak
description: This cloak shimmers and sparkles, making the image of the wearer unclear, acting as a constant Blur (Guises) effect. It is a x2 PP Multiplier for Mystics, and it allows the wearer to use Reverse Spell I (Spell Reins) once each day.
recipe: Work Mundane Cloth (2), Daily X (25), Embed X (19), Reverse Spell I (10), Power Multiplier IV (25 x 2 hybrid x 2 wearable), Constant III (17), Embed I (3), Blur (1). Days: 177. Standard cost: 5,487sp. Level 25 item.

name: Skeleton Key of Opening
description: This iron key has a constant Opening I (Unbarring Ways) effect on it. It can only be tried on any given lock once: if it succeeds in opening that lock, it will always work on that lock. If it fails, it will never open that lock.
recipe: Work Lesser Metals (5), Constant X (35), Embed IV (9), Opening I (4). Days: 53. Standard cost: 2,014sp. Level 35 item.

name: Skull of Rie-talin
description: Rie-talin was a powerful Evil Sorcerer, but when he attempted to cheat death by becoming a lich, it all went horribly wrong. He died, and only his skull was left behind, now nigh indestructible and endowed with power. When held, it serves as a x2 PP Multiplier, a +4 Spell Adder, and a +20 SCR device for Sorcerers. Legends say that if it were to be destroyed, the destroyer would gain powerful protection against Sorcerers.
recipe: Work Organic (6), Unbreakable Work (90), Unbreakable Work (90), Power Multiplier IV (25 x 2 hybrid), Spell Adder IV (19 x 2 hybrid), Casting Focus IV (15 x 2 hybrid). Days: 304. Standard cost: 30,096sp. Level 90 item.

name: Staff of Healing
description: This staff of warm yellow wood (equivalent to oak) is quite plain in appearance, but powerful. It is equivalent to a half-bole and two staves. The half-bole ability provides 200 charges of Undisease or Unpoison (Purifications), while the two staff abilities offer 100 charges of Sense Restoration (Nerve and Organ Mastery) and 100 charges of Heal V (Concussion Mastery).
recipe: Work Mundane Wood (4), Make Half-Bole (19), Size Reduction (13), Make Staff (14), Make Staff (14), Embed XX (35), Embed XX (35), Unpoison (15), Undisease (14), Logic Fork (1), Embed X (20), Heal V (8), Embed VII (17), Sense Restoration (6). Days: 215. Standard cost: 9,070sp. Level 35 item.

name: Staff of Life
description: This 7' staff has an elaborate upper end in the form of a flattened representation of a great tree (Str 75, 6 lbs., 12 sp). It provides the bearer access to the Life Mastery list regardless of his own Realm and acts as a +7 Spell Adder for healing spells. Once each day, it may reroll a failed breakage roll.
recipe: Work Mundane Wood (4), Consecration (2), Emplace List True (90), Adaptive Power (7), Sacred Adder VII (30), Resilience (15). Days: 148. Standard cost: 14,072sp. Level 90 item.

name: Staff of Light’s Echoes
description: This is a walking staff of twisted ironwood. It is a +9 Spell Adder for both Channeling and Essence. Channeling and Essence users will also gain a +35 bonus to all SCRs. The user can cast Word of Return once a month, Rereturning once a fortnight, and Store Sanctuary (all Lofty Movements) once per week. It also allows the wielder to cast spells up to 20th level from the Spell Reins list, regardless of his Realm.
recipe: Work Mundane Wood (4), Daily XX (50), Embed True (60), Rereturning (50), Spell Adder X (70 x 2 two Realms), Casting Focus VII (35 x 2 two Realms), Lord Emplace List (50), Daily XL (75), Embed True (60), Word of Return (30), Daily X (25), Embed X (20), Store Sanctuary (8), Adaptive Power (7). Days: 649. Standard cost: 70,292sp. Level 90 item.

name: Staff of Sorcery
description: This is a x2 PP Multiplier for Sorcerers and provides a +15 RR bonus against spells cast by Hybrid spellcasters. This oak staff is topped with a rock crystal sphere (80 sp). It is a walking staff, not balanced for fighting, and any attempt to use it for melee will be at -20.
recipe: Work Mundane Wood (4), Consecration (2), Power Multiplier IV (25), Sacred Protections IV (14). Days: 45. Standard cost would be 1,340sp, but spell failure reduced the RR bonus, so price drops to 1,206sp. Level 25 item.

name: Staff of the Coast Runners
description: This elm staff provides the power of a half-bole with the effect of either Command Current (Water Law) or Reverse Wind (Wind Law). Furthermore, the bearer is the target of a constant Waterrunning (Moving Ways) spell.
recipe: Work Mundane Wood (4), Make Half-Bole (19), Size Reduction (13), Embed XX (30), Embed XX (30), Reverse Winds (20), Command Current (17), Logic Fork (1), Constant X (40), Embed X (20), Waterrunning (9). Days: 203. Standard cost: 9,541sp. Level 40 item.

name: Staff of the Winds
description: This vaal staff is beautifully carved with images of clouds, wind spirits, birds, and all manner of winged creatures. It would be worth 800 sp unenchanted. It provides the wielder with constant powers of Levitation (Lofty Bridge), Fly I (Lofty Bridge), and Wind Control (Fluid Elements). The wielder may also invoke Slumber Mist (Wind Law) six times per day and Death Cloud I (Wind Law) four times per day.
recipe: Work Wood (18), Constant True (90), Embed XX (30), Wind Control (18), Daily L (75), Embed XX (30), Death Cloud I (11), Constant X (35), Embed V (11), Fly I (5), Daily XX (40), Embed III (7), Slumber Mist (3), Constant X (35), Embed IV (9), Levitation (4). Days: 421. Standard cost: 43,742sp. Level 90 item.

name: Throne of Trent
description: This enormous throne is made of basalt set with a multitude of precious stones, making it sparkle like the starriest night. It is known by many names, depending on which of its previous owners is most locally famous. Weighing five tons, valued at 100,000 sp base on its material worth alone, it is most notable for the many powers it grants the one seated upon it (and attuned to its power, of course). Each of the following spells is available for casting on a daily basis: Wall of Force, Spell Shield True, Lord Summons, Fly III, and Teleport I. A Fire Bolt I spell may be cast three times each day. A constant See Invisible I is provided, while Delving and Detect Evil are available for use at will. The throne’s interaction with the attuned user’s aura is especially strong (due, it is believed, to properties of the many gems), allowing the Fly III and Teleport I spells to encompass the great mass of the throne as if it were the weight of the caster when determining how much may be carried along by these spells. Since daily Wall of Force is not available from an Adept, this capability is assumed to come from a Sanctifier.
recipe: Work Mundane Gemstone (8), Consecration (2), Wall of Force (50, Barrier Law), Sacred Spell True (75), Delving (10, Delving Ways), Embed X (19), Constant X (35 x 2 for at will), Spell Shield True (18, Spell Wall), Embed XX (30), Daily XX (40), Lord Summons (16, Gate Mastery), Embed XX (30), Daily XX (40), Fly III (11, Lofty Bridge), Embed XX (30), Daily XX (40), See Invisible I (9, Invisible Ways), Embed X (19), Constant X (35), Fire Bolt I (6, Fire Law), Embed VI (13), Daily XX (40), Teleport I (10, Lofty Bridge), Embed X (19), Daily X (25), Detect Evil (2, Lore), Embed II (6), Constant III (18 x 2 for at will). Days: 709. Standard cost: 166,646sp. Level 75 item. Note that the listed value does not include the artistic or historical value of the throne. Such a price is pretty much a theoretical valuing in any case. This is a typical ‘priceless’ item, and any exchange is unlikely to be in the form of money alone. Skull of Rie-talin

name: Wand of +10 Shock Bolts
description: This is a wand of ash, forked at the business end. As one would guess from the name, it provides Shock Bolt I, with a +10 bonus to the OB.
recipe: Work Mundane Wood (4), Make Wand (7), Embed II (5), Shock Bolt I (2), Weapon II (8, doubled for Directed Spell weapon). Days: 34. Standard cost: 340sp. Level 8 item. 6.9. OTHER These are items that do not fit into the above categories.

name: Wand of Fire Balls
description: This plain wand of ash wood has the power of a magical staff, reduced to a conveniently-carried and concealable size. Compare this to a regular staff of Fire Balls, which would only require only 45 days and cost 990sp. The convenience of compact size has a considerable price. The highest level spell required in either case is Embed X, so both the normal staff and the wand-sized version are both 19th level items.
recipe: Work Mundane Wood (4), Make Staff (14), Size Reduction (13), Size Reduction (13), Embed X (19), Fire Ball (8 from Fire Law). Days: 71. Standard cost: 1,562sp. Level 19 item.

name: Wand of Lightning
description: This vaal wand is an undersized staff providing a Lightning Bolt I (Light Law) spell. The wand provides a +10 OB bonus to those bolts.
recipe: Work Wood (18), Make Staff (14), Size Reduction (13), Size Reduction (13), Weapon II (8 x 2 Directed Spell), Embed X (19), Lightning Bolt I (10). Days: 103. Standard cost: 2,969sp. Level 19 item.

name: Wand of Sleep
description: This ash wand provides a Sleep III (level 1 on the Spirit Mastery spell list) spell. It requires
recipe: Work Mundane Wood (level 4), Make Wand (level 7), Embed I (3), and Sleep III (1). It will require 15 days of work, for a cost of 135sp. It is a level 7 item.

name: Armor-Slaying Sword
description: This +10 claymore is made of Low Steel with a plain leather grip. The blade is inscribed "Let metal eat metal". It has the Armor Breaker and Shield Breaker abilities.
recipe: Work Mundane Metal I (7), Armor Breaker (18), Shield Breaker (12), Weapon II (8). Days: 40. Standard cost: 901sp. Level 18 item.

name: Assassin Slayer
description: This high steel (+10) arming sword is Slaying III against Magents and Slaying II against Thieves. It provides a +20 bonus to Reverse Strike skill.
recipe: Work Mundane Metal II (9), Slaying III (40), General IV (30), Slaying II (20). Days: 89. Standard cost: 4,034sp. Level 40 item.

name: Axe of Foul Summons
description: This +10 long axe has a head of High Steel etched with the design of a summoning circle. The oaken haft is stained with irregular dark blotches. Once per day, the wielder may cast Summon Demon IV, with a 90% chance that Control Demon V will be cast at the same time.
recipe: Work Mundane Metal II (9), Embed XX (35), Daily XX (50), Control Demon V (20), Embed XX (35), Daily XX (50), Summon Demon IV (15). Days: 204. Standard cost: 10,571sp (would be 11,745sp, but the unreliability of Control Demon casting reduces marketability). Level 50 item.

name: Axe of Orc Slaying
description: Made from Dwarven High Steel and stout hickory, this battle axe has a +15 bonus except against Orcs. Against Orcs it is +25 and Slaying I. Dwarven High Steel improves the strength of the weapon (for breakage purposes) to 95, increased by the level of the enchantment to 110.
recipe: Work Mundane Metal II (9), Slaying I (15), Weapon III (13), Weapon II (8 x 1/2 Variety). Days: 31. Standard cost: 771sp. Level 15 item.

name: Beamguard’s Staff
description: This hickory (+10) quarterstaff allows its wielder to cast Fire Ball (Fire Law) once per week, spellmastered to exclude the wielder from the area of effect, allowing it to be used at close range. It also provides a +20 Grace bonus for both the Fire Law and Luminous Elements lists.
recipe: Work Mundane Wood (4), General IV (30), General IV (30), Daily X (25), Embed X (19), Fire Ball (8). Days: 116. Standard cost: 3,704sp. Level 30 item.

name: Veils of Tanu
description: Three gauzy veils glittering with tiny diamonds (300 sp), this item allows the wearer to cast Face Shifting True (Mystical Change) five times per day and provides a constant Beautify (Living Change) effect so long as the veils cover the wearer’s face.
recipe: Work Mundane Gemstone (8), Face Shifting True (2), Hybrid Embedding (2), Embed II (6), Daily X (25), Beautify (2), Embed II (5), Constant III (17). Days: 67. Standard cost: 2,176sp. Level 25 item.

name: Witch’s Eye
description: This silver necklace has an eye-shaped pendant with a heliotrope "pupil" (total value 210 sp). It is a +1 Spell Adder for Druids and gives the wearer the Petrifying Gaze of a gorgon (50 DP talent, see Creature Law).
recipe: Work Mundane Gemstone (8), Talent Embedding True (75), Spell Adder I (5 x 1/2 single profession). Days: 86. Standard cost: 7,004sp. Level 75 item.
